title: Monday standup
question: Give your Monday standup in 60 seconds: what you shipped last week, what you're focused on this week, one blocker.

title: Meeting wrap-up
question: Wrap up a meeting in 30 seconds — summarize what was decided and one next step.

title: Blocker statement
question: What are you blocked on right now, and what would unblock you?

title: Async update
question: Drop a 60-second async update for a teammate who was out yesterday — what they missed and what's still in flight.

title: End-of-week recap
question: Recap your week in 60 seconds: one win, one challenge, one thing carrying into next week.

title: Risk flag
question: Flag a risk to your team in two sentences — what could slip and why.

title: Pitch the product
question: Explain what your product or project actually does, like you're showing it to a friend, in 60 seconds.
framework: What it is → who it's for → what changes for them.

title: Role intro for a new hire
question: Explain what you do in your role to a new hire from another department, in 60 seconds.
framework: What you do → who relies on it → typical week.

title: How it works
question: Walk through how your product works, end-to-end, in under a minute.

title: What changed this quarter
question: In 60 seconds, walk a stakeholder through what shipped on your project this quarter and what's next.

title: Architecture overview
question: Give a 60-second architecture overview of the system you work on most.

title: Tradeoff explanation
question: Explain one tradeoff you made on your current project and why, in 45 seconds.

title: Pitch the project
question: Pitch your current project in 60 seconds to someone outside your team.
framework: Why it matters → what it does → what changes.

title: Make the case
question: Make the case in 60 seconds for a change you actually want at work — what to change, why, and what improves.

title: Buy-in from leadership
question: In 60 seconds, ask a senior leader to back your initiative — what you need from them, and why now.

title: Trade-off recommendation
question: Recommend one trade-off your team should make this quarter and why, in under a minute.

title: Defend the priority
question: You have a case for prioritizing your current work over what your team is pushing for — make it in 60 seconds.

title: One-sentence pitch
question: Pitch what your team is working on in one sentence — then expand for 30 more seconds.

title: Respectful disagreement
question: Disagree with a teammate's idea respectfully in 2 sentences.

title: Deadline pushback
question: Tell your manager you can't ship by Friday — keep it under 30 seconds.

title: Direct feedback
question: Give a teammate direct feedback about something they did well — and one thing to do differently — in 60 seconds.

title: Saying no
question: Decline a request from a stakeholder in two sentences without burning the relationship.

title: Bad news to your team
question: Deliver a piece of bad news to your team in 45 seconds — what happened and what's next.

title: Pushback to a senior
question: Push back on a senior person's call in 30 seconds — name the concern and propose an alternative.

title: All-hands intro
question: Introduce yourself at a new team's all-hands in 30 seconds.
framework: Name → role → one thing about you.

title: Networking intro
question: Introduce yourself like we just met at a conference.

title: Tell me about yourself
question: Walk me through your background in 60 seconds — what you do and why.

title: Coffee chat opener
question: You're starting a coffee chat with someone you want to learn from. Open in 30 seconds.

title: Elevator pitch
question: Give your 30-second elevator pitch — who you are and what you're working on.

title: Why this work
question: Why are you doing the work you're doing right now? Answer in 60 seconds.

title: One strength
question: Name one strength you bring at work — and a moment that proved it — in 60 seconds.

title: Career inflection
question: Talk through a moment that changed how you work, in 60 seconds.

title: What you'd do over
question: If you could redo one decision from the last year, what would it be and why? 60 seconds.

title: What you're learning
question: What are you learning right now and why does it matter to you? 45 seconds.

title: Project you led
question: In 60 seconds, talk about a project you led — outcome first, process second.

title: Pushed back
question: Tell me about a time you had to push back on a stakeholder. What happened? 60 seconds.

title: Hard feedback
question: Tell me about a time you got hard feedback. What did you do with it? 60 seconds.

title: Failed and recovered
question: Tell me about a time something you owned failed — and what you did next. 60 seconds.
framework: What happened → what you did → what you learned.

title: Drove alignment
question: Walk me through a time you got people aligned on something hard. 60 seconds.

title: Cross-team work
question: Tell me about working across teams to ship something. 60 seconds.

title: Missed sprint
question: What would you do if a teammate repeatedly missed sprint commitments?

title: Productivity opinion
question: What do you think about engineering productivity? 60 seconds.

title: First 30 days
question: What would you focus on in your first 30 days in this role?

title: Conflicting priorities
question: Two senior people give you conflicting priorities. What do you do? 60 seconds.

title: Team falling behind
question: Your team is falling behind on a critical deliverable. Walk me through how you'd respond.

title: Feedback culture
question: What do you think makes feedback actually land at work? 60 seconds.
